message:
test(schema,id-hash): cover v7 content-hash rebuild + id-hash parity holds under the new algorithm

Two regression tests locking invariants of the content-hash alignment:

src/storage/schema.rs:
- `test_v7_rebuilds_content_hashes_and_marks_dirty` seeds a v6 DB
  with an `old-rust-hash` placeholder in `content_hash`, an issue
  row in each of the relevant statuses, triggers the v7 migration,
  and asserts:
    * Every row's `content_hash` is overwritten with the Go-parity
      value computed from its typed fields.
    * The issue rows are marked dirty via the JSONL-tracking
      metadata table so the next `br sync --flush-only` rewrites
      them.
    * `PRAGMA user_version` advances to 7.
  Locks the three invariants the rebuild path is responsible for —
  any future refactor that skips the dirty-mark step will fail here
  instead of shipping out-of-date JSONL.

tests/storage_id_hash_parity.rs:
- Add a targeted assertion that generating an issue ID from the
  new content-hash bytes still yields the same length-prefixed
  base36 ID as before the algorithm change. The ID generator uses
  the FIRST N bytes of the hash, so even a one-bit change to the
  hash input would produce a different ID. This test exercises the
  full pipeline: construct an Issue, compute its content_hash with
  the Go-parity algorithm, feed that hash into
  `id_from_content_hash`, and assert the resulting ID starts with
  the expected prefix and matches the length the planner picked.

Together with the fixture test in conformance.rs and the proptest
parity in proptest_hash.rs, the Go-parity algorithm now has
coverage at four levels: unit (proptest), fixture (conformance),
storage migration (schema), and pipeline (id-hash parity).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
